MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION .
Off Calendar Jul-27-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: James Claytor for (petitioner) David Ravetti. Phil Foster for (respondent) Mary Ravetti.

The Court confirmed that we are here today on the Summary Judgment and Summary Adjudication, stated that as to the discovery motion the tentative is confirmed and that Mr. Foster is to provide a proposed order.

Mr. Claytor presented oral argument citing the Dobler decisions regarding failure to disburse trust funds, cited 19000 & 19001(a) which he read to the court and stated that on 2/18/14 a probate number was assigned when the responded filed her claim. The Court stated that nothing says that a claim cannot be brought. Mr. Claytor cited 19001 as to the insufficiency of probate assets to pay a claim, cited P.C. 19253 regarding claims barred by the statute of limitations, that there must be a compliant optional claims procedure, that there is no case that has interpreted this situation, that respondent opened probate and filed but did not publish to start the tolling. The Court stated that it is clear that if you want to create the 4 month cutoff you have to follow the requirements, that the claimant is not penalized by the trustee's failure to publish and asked what if it was a 3rd party claimant. Mr. Claytor stated that there are no other creditors, that the trustee and claimant are the same and that he is unable to cite a case or statute. The Court stated that it does not mean that the process cannot be invoked. Mr. Claytor stated that this is not a 3rd party, that the trustee was waiting until the 11th hour, that the trustee's action had no benefit to the estate, that it was only for her own benefit and that the idea is that the trustee, for the benefit of the beneficiaries is to promptly ascertain the claims and pay them. The Court stated that counsel's argument is with the legislature. Mr. Claytor cited 19040 regarding lack of publication and cited 19051 and read it to the court. The Court cited 19253(b) stating that tolling is dependent on successful notice. Mr. Claytor also cited 19253(b) stating that part of it follows from the process that the trustee failed to comply with and that the whole process never came to a point where the tolling could take place. Mr. Foster cited 19051 stating that the notice referred to is the actual notice to creditors, which was complied with and that 1201 relieves the trustee from serving herself. The Court stated that the tentatives are confirmed and that Mr. Foster is to prepare proposed orders.

The Court referred to the motion to seal which was taken off calendar and asked counsel if that pleading will now be filed. Mr. Claytor responded that they had submitted it but that it was rejected, that he thought they had an agreement on filing it and that he will confer with Mr. Foster but will not be filing it under seal.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. Court Reporter: April Heveroh, CSR #8759, 510-406-6651 (Reported).

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: James D. Claytor for petitioner David Ravetti; Ernest Der for Debra Dolch; David Brillant and Joseph Russionello for Mary Ravetti.

The Court inquired wether the parties had agreed on a professional fiduciary for appointment as temporary trustee. Mr. Claytor informed the Court that there was still no agreement on the selection of a temporary trustee. His preference was for the appointment of Mr. Statman to serve in that capacity, who was available and ready to serve so long as partnership issues would not be raised. He stated that he had also spoken with Ms. Dolch, who likewise had informed him that she would not agree to serve if the duties of the temporary trustee went beyond handling the sale of the property. Mr. Der confirmed that Ms. Dolch would be willing to serve as trustee only in that limited capacity. The Court expressed concern regarding the course the matter has taken. Mr. Brillant stated that Ms. Ravetti has been cooperating under the Court's order and has exchanged names for a prospective trustee as litem. Mr.Claytor stated that it would be awkward having two trustees, and that there were conflict of interest issues which required a new impartial trustee. He stated that one issue of conflict was the claim by Ms. Ravetti for caregiving services given by her, and another was a $2,000,000 holdback from distribution of proceeds of sale. Mr. Brillant stated that it was not unusual to have 2 or more trusteew, and asserted that it would be more expensive to bring in a trustee for all purposes and bring them up to speed. He added that valuation issues could be addressed at a 17200 proceeding providing court review. Mr.Russionello stated that he was a longtime neighbor and friend of the Ravetti family, and that from his review of the records it appeared to him that everything Mary Ravetti had done as trustee was done in the best interests of the trust.
The Court stated that he was not going to expand the scope of suspension of the trustees powers at this time, but he was granting the request to appoint Debra Dolch as Trustee ad Litem will all powers appropriate for the sale of the commercial property. Mr. Brillant volunteered to prepare an appropriate order. The three petitions before the Court were ordered continued to August 2, 2016, at 9:00 am in Department 204, and a status hearing regarding the petitions and trial preparation is set for the same time and date. Any supplemental sumissions or statements to be considered by the Court at the status hearing are to be provided to the Court by July 26, 2016. The trial on the petitions is set for September 12, 2016 at 9:30 am in Department 206.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: James Claytor for (petitioner) David Ravetti. John Stein for (trustee) Mary Ravetti.

The Court asked what was happening with the property. Mr. Stein reported that the commercial property was listed on 9/18/15, that offers are trickling in, that they expect it to take 3-6 months from open to close, that they requested that the broker get the property sold. Mr. Stein reported that the residential property was listed on 9/30/15, that there was an open house on 10/4/15, that there is a brokers tour today, that interest is being shown on the property, that there will be further open house dates if necessary and that they will follow the brokers recommendation. Mr. Claytor stated that the commercial property does not have a listing price, that commercial property takes longer to sell, that they want to be kept abreast of offers made, track what is going on and how many people have requested confidentiality agreements. Mr. Stein stated that they receive periodic marketing updates from the broker and that he will forward these to Mr. Claytor as they come in. Mr. Claytor confirmed that this is acceptable and requested updates on the residential property as well. Mr. Stein stated that he will send what is available and that he expects it to sell fast. The Court requested that Mr. Claytor updated in a timely manner so that he has confidence in what is being done and asked if there was a mortgage on the residence. Mr. Stein reported that the mortgage is 1.4 or 1.5 million. Mr. Claytor stated that there were advances made from the commercial property to the trust and that there is an obligation on the part of 110 to pay back certain advances and that when the residence sells it will flow back to the limited partnership for advances made. The Court stated that other topics are what becomes of the distributable income of the properties and how to resolve the remaining claims pending, that it had hoped that the meet and confer regarding the reserve could be more productive, that it needs to accommodate the litigation and that 1.4 million is a lot of money to reserve. Mr. Stein stated that regarding the 1.4 million there are 4 petitions and a complaint, quite a bit of litigation as well as the ongoing administration of the trust and taxes. The Court stated that they will have to come up with a figure at some point, that Mr. Claytor's client who will want money has been waiting a long time, that there could have been a speedier movement to this point, that they will need information on what is really needed to hold with distributing as much as possible. Mr. Claytor stated that there is a petition to instruct trustee to reject claim and an ex parte petition to removed and that they should be continued. The Court stated that it will not suspend the trustee while the properties are being actively marketed, and that the other issue of resolving the trustees claim will have to be litigated here and suggested that the parties bring motions. Mr. Stein stated that the matters should be teed up together with limited briefs. The Court stated that they may need to agree to have a motion for summary adjudication decided if the parties and court agree. Mr. Claytor stated that litigation of the merits of $588,000.00 would be costly and would like it deferred. The Court stated that the motions would be heard logically together if they are logically connected. The petition to compel trustee to provide an accounting, etc., the petition for order instructing trustee, etc., and the petition for order removing trustee, etc., were continued to November 17, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status on the progress of the properties, the ex parte request to suspend, the petition regarding trustees claim and that the parties are to meet and confer and figure out a way to present to the court a plan to proceed.

Petition To Compel Trustee To Provide An Accounting; Determine Validity Of Trust Provision;Etc/
Off Calendar Sep-02-2015 Continued To Oct-06-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: James Claytor for (petitioner) David Ravetti. John Stein for (respondent/trustee) Mary Ravetti.

The Court stated that it had ordered the commercial property to be listed, that it was listed but quickly withdrawn and that the court had been told that the trustee was on board with selling the property. Mr. Stein stated that the goal is still the same regarding the commercial property, that the property had been listed but there was not much interest in it, that it will be back on the market this month, that in regards to the residential property they found a more efficient way to do this, that it will save the estate money and that if the issue had been identified but not raised there would have been a surcharge. Mr. Claytor stated that there has been a petition filed to cause suspension of the trustee which will be heard this Friday, that there was an order issued in April to list the commercial property, that they question why it was listed at the particular price, that it seems it would have been prudent to let the beneficiaries know, that they found out by a random internet search, that in regards to 110 there is an outstanding court order and that facts known prior to the April order have caused the trustee to decide she wants the property. The Court stated that if the trustee realized that a mistake was made she had a duty to clear it and asked if any effort had been made for the parties to communicate. Mr. Stein stated that they had a meet and confer but that they did not reach out regarding the property. The Court stated that it was baffling given all that has happened that the trustee would not reach out to let everyone know what was happening. Mr. Stein replied that the first priority was to get the building sold at the best price, that it was within the power of the trustee to make these decisions and that they were not looking for a fight. Mr. Claytor stated that they have filed a petition for instructions and a petition for removal, that they are set for September 29, 2015 before another Judicial Officer. The Court stated that it makes sense for all of the issues to be heard together and continued the petition to compel trustee to provide an accounting, etc., as well as the petition for order removing trustee and the petition for instructions currently set for 9/29/15 to October 6, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court instructed the parties to go out into the hall and talk, that it is clear that there is not enough communication regarding the administration of this trust and that everyone needs to be on the same page. Mr. Stein asked how to move forward in regards to the home. The court responded that it had made its order, that it does not know what their information is and does not know the procedural issues. Mr. Claytor asked about the savings of capital gains and distribution. The Court stated that there are many ways to deal with that, that it depends on the parties willingness to implement.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

Petition To Compel Trustee To Provide An Accounting; Determine Validity Of Trust Provision;Etc/
Off Calendar Jun-23-2015 Continued To Jul-28-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: James Claytor for (petitioner) David Ravetti. John Stein for (respondent) Mary Ravetti.

Mr. Stein reported that the commercial property is on track to be sold for June 30, 2015 and that they have signed a broker. The Court stated that there the issue of the residential property, that there are experts with different views on timing and that the trustee needs to make a decision based on what is best for the trust. Mr. Claytor stated that the residential property has been vacant since 2013, that rental income could be $10,000.00 per month, that waiting until September loses income and that non productivity continues. The court stated that it is not the courts position to override the trustee, that the trustee has no interest in undercutting value to the trust and asked if the parties would be able to talk and work out the issues pertaining to creditor claims and holdbacks that would go into distribution. Mr. Stein stated that they have met and conferred and that he believes the $1,000,000.00 reserve is reasonable given what is and could be outstanding. Mr. Claytor stated that he is unsatisfied in regards to the $1,000,000.00 holdback, would like to know how the $588,000.00 is to be paid and would have liked an opportunity to discuss the $1,000,000.00 holdback before now. The court stated that some of this may not be properly before the court, that the objectors should have a process of how to address the creditors claim and that there should be a way to meet and confer regarding this and any other issues that need to be litigated. Mr. Claytor stated that he concurs that the merits of the creditor claim are not before the court, that it was raised at the last hearing that it would be paid and that he would like to meet with counsel regarding the $1,000,000.00 holdback. Mr. Stein reported that they intend to pay the claim in full. The court stated that it will continue the matter one more time for the parties to meet and discuss the issues here today, the marketing of the properties, the prompt payout after sales and what is left and how to resolve it. The Petition to compel trustee to provide an accounting, etc., was continued to July 28, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

Off Calendar Apr-07-2015 Continued To Jun-09-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: James Claytor for (petitioner) David Ravetti. Jack Bonanno for (respondent & trustee) Mary Ravetti.

Mr. Bonanno reported that he e-filed a status report yesterday and provided a copy to the court. Mr. Claytor stated that he received the plan for distribution yesterday, that the plan calls for the residential property to be listed for sale by September 2015, the commercial property to be listed for sale by June 2015 and the personal property to be distributed. Mr. Claytor stated that the listing in June is reasonable, is not in agreement with waiting until September to list the residential property and that the personal property should be distributed right away. Mr. Bonanno clarified that the residential property would be listed no later than September 2015, that the trustee is being advised to wait until it is opportune to sell the property, that the timing is not indefinite, that there will be preliminary distribution as each property is sold, that the personal property involves the trustee and grandchildren only and is not before the court and that there is currently no calculation as to what reserves will need to be held. Mr. Claytor stated that the report gives no reason to hold up the sale on the residential property, that this is the time to start listing and spoke of a pending claim and malpractice litigation not currently before the court. The court stated that it is inclined to order the commercial property by listed for sale by June 2015, the residential property to be listed for sale by September 2015, with a status date regarding the order for June 9, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to confirm that the commercial property has been listed and status on the residential property listing, that distribution of proceeds of the sales should be as prompt as possible, but no later that 3 months subject to reasonable reserves and hold backs. Mr. Bonanno is to prepare the order. The petition to compel trustee to provide an accounting, etc. was continued to June 9, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)
